title: symphytum officinale var. patens
intro: Symphytum officinale var. patens, also known as Comfrey, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and native to Europe and Asia. Comfrey has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which make it beneficial for wound healing, reducing pain and swelling, and promoting tissue repair. The health benefits of Comfrey include alleviating symptoms of osteoarthritis, gout, and other musculoskeletal conditions, as well as supporting liver and digestive health. Comfrey is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, tinctures, salves, and creams, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
